 {"id":"bd-27t","title":"E2E CLI scenarios (print/continue/resume/export/packages) with artifact logging","description":"# Goal
Own the CLI E2E suite as a rollup: coordinate scenario tasks and ensure unified logging + artifact capture.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Aggregate scenario tasks: print/stdin (bd-1ub), session lifecycle (bd-idw), tool enable/disable + error paths (bd-1o4).
- Ensure shared harness/env isolation and logging spec are applied consistently.
- Provide a single entrypoint script/runner to execute the full CLI E2E suite and collect artifacts.

# Acceptance
- All child scenario tasks completed and wired into a single CLI E2E suite run.
- Logs follow unified JSONL spec and artifact index.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:51.748950351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:36.774727141Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1o4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-c4q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-idw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Expand CLI E2E: print mode, @file context, --continue/--resume, session export HTML, package list/install/remove (local/global), config paths, model listing. Capture stdout/stderr + timing + session artifacts per run.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:20:17Z"},{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rollup note: treat bd-1ub, bd-idw, bd-1o4 as the scenario tasks; this issue owns the unified CLI E2E runner and ensures JSONL log spec (bd-4u9) is applied across all scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28rk","title":"Autocomplete: core provider (commands/prompts/skills/files/paths)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core autocomplete provider that produces suggestions based on editor text + cursor position.

# Suggestions required (legacy parity)
- Slash commands: built-in commands (`/help`, `/model`, `/settings`, etc.).
- Prompt templates: `/<template>` from ResourceLoader.
- Skills: `/skill:<name>` when enableSkillCommands is true.
- File reference: when user types `@`, fuzzy-search project files and insert selected path.
- Path completion: when cursor is in a path-like token, produce candidates (use `fd` if available).

# Matching
- Fuzzy matching for file and command lists.
- Stable ordering (deterministic): exact prefix matches first, then fuzzy score.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep provider logic independent of rendering.
- Provider should return a structured `AutocompleteItem`:
  - display label
  - insert text
  - optional description
  - kind (command/template/skill/file/path)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provider can be called with (text, cursor) and returns stable suggestions.
- [ ] Provider includes file suggestions without scanning the entire filesystem on every keystroke (cache or incremental search).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.177076040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441887518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441818900Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28t8","title":"Autocomplete: UI dropdown + selection/insertion","description":"# Goal
Add an interactive autocomplete dropdown to the editor, matching the core UX of legacy Pi.

# Required Behavior
- Trigger conditions:
  - typing `/` shows command/template suggestions
  - typing `@` shows file suggestions
  - Tab can also open/advance path completion
- Navigation:
  - Up/Down (or configured selection keys)
  - Enter/Tab to accept
  - Escape/Ctrl+C to cancel
- Display:
  - max visible items respects `autocomplete_max_visible` config
  - show item kind (icon or color) and optional description

# Implementation Notes
- Use bubbles list-like UI or a small custom component.
- Keep insertion logic robust:
  - insert at cursor
  - replace the active token prefix (e.g., replace `@foo` with `@src/foo.rs` or just `src/foo.rs` depending on chosen syntax)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Autocomplete can be opened, navigated, accepted, cancelled.
- [ ] Acceptance inserts the correct text at the correct location.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:18.250299287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:36:32.531381385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:36:32.531316274Z","close_reason":"Implemented autocomplete UI dropdown with navigation, selection, and rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-28rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-28uy","title":"Interactive: /copy copies last assistant message to clipboard","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/copy` behavior: copy last assistant message text to the system clipboard.

# Required Behavior
- If there is no assistant message yet: show an error (“No agent messages to copy yet.” or equivalent).
- On success: show a short status (“Copied …”).
- On failure: show an actionable error (clipboard unavailable).

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a cross-platform clipboard crate with minimal optional dependencies.
- Avoid feature-gating the entire command away; at minimum provide a runtime fallback that prints the text or writes it to a temp file with a clear message.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/copy` works out-of-the-box on macOS/Linux/Windows where clipboard is available.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.932575094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:27:02.608882453Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28uy","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-29c","title":"Workstream: popular extension sample selection + acquisition complete","description":"Purpose:
- Produce a *large, diverse, popular* extension sample with frozen versions and archived artifacts so conformance tests are repeatable.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Sample selection criteria + sampling matrix (documented).
- Final manifest of chosen extensions (name, source, version, checksum, capabilities, categories).
- Archived artifacts (tarballs/zip) stored under a dedicated fixtures area with checksums.

Definition of done:
- Sample list is final and frozen with versions.
- Artifacts are locally archived with checksums.
- License/redistribution policy applied to each artifact (OK / restricted / exclude).

Dependencies:
- This workstream blocks legacy capture, harness, and reporting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:44.802403431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:28:15.393814835Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-22h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-2uv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-2wo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-3rr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-gx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b3","title":"Resource loader + package manager tests with real fixtures","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:12.631899363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:19:34.391872711Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b3","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b3","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-2b3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cover resource discovery precedence (project > global), CLI overrides (--extension/--no-extensions), and manifest-driven resources.  {"id":"bd-2c2r","title":"Settings: message delivery (steering/follow-up modes)","description":"# Goal
Expose message delivery settings in `/settings`:
- steeringMode
- followUpMode

# Allowed Values
- `one-at-a-time` (default)
- `all`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can change delivery modes via `/settings`.
- [ ] Changes persist and affect message queue behavior.

# Dependencies
- Depends on message queue config wiring (`bd-k7ke`).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:39.572399995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:19.589133636Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c2r","depends_on_id":"bd-1cd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2r","depends_on_id":"bd-2mcr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2r","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2r","depends_on_id":"bd-k7ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ds","title":"Connector: HTTP/network minimal API (policy-gated)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **minimal HTTP/network connector** with strict capability policy and timeouts.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Basic fetch: GET/POST with headers/body.
- Host allowlist/denylist + TLS required by default.
- Request timeouts and size limits.
- Optional streaming with backpressure (if needed).
- Structured logs for each request (host, method, duration, outcome).

# Security Invariants
- Network access only to allowed hosts and methods.
- No implicit proxying or ambient DNS access beyond policy.

# Tests
- Unit tests for denied host -> deterministic error.
- Timeout path -> deterministic mapping + log assertion.
- E2E security suite validates policy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:37.350974293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:24.555143471Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:24.555077217Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ds","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ds","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-2ds","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"HTTP connector implementation complete:

**Files created/modified:**
- src/connectors/mod.rs - Connector trait + hostcall protocol types (self-contained)
- src/connectors/http.rs - Full HTTP connector (~670 lines)

**Features implemented:**
- HttpConnectorConfig: allowlist/denylist, TLS requirement, size limits, timeouts
- URL validation against policy (TLS check, allowlist/denylist pattern matching)
- Wildcard host pattern matching (*.example.com)
- Request parsing and validation (method, body size)
- Request execution via existing http::Client
- Response body streaming with size limits
- Text/binary body handling (UTF-8 with base64 fallback)
- Structured tracing logs for audit trail

**Tests:** 11 unit tests passing:
- Default config validation
- TLS required/not required
- Allowlist/denylist matching
- Denylist precedence over allowlist
- Pattern matching (wildcards, case insensitivity)
- Request parsing (valid, invalid method, body too large)
- Config serialization roundtrip

All tests pass, code compiles cleanly.
","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2glw","title":"Docs: packages.md (install/remove/update/list + resource resolution)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/packages.md` documenting package sources and CLI subcommands.

# Source Material
- Legacy: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/packages.md`
- Rust: `src/package_manager.rs`, `src/cli.rs`

# Must Include
- Supported sources (npm/git/local) as implemented.
- `pi install/remove/update/list/config` usage.
- How package resources (skills/prompts/themes/extensions) are discovered.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:50:04.105877443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:50:04.115349984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2glw","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gn","title":"Unit tests: ResourceLoader discovery + diagnostics","description":"Goal:
- Add unit/integration tests for ResourceLoader (skills/prompts/themes/extensions) using real files and directories, with detailed logging.

Scope:
- Skill discovery from explicit paths + defaults.
- Prompt template discovery + metadata parsing.
- Theme loading with explicit path + package sources.
- Diagnostics: collisions, invalid files, missing resources, precedence rules.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) in each test.
- Log resolved paths, chosen winners for collisions, and diagnostics payloads.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests use real temp dirs and files; no mocks.
- Diagnostics asserted for collisions + invalid metadata.
- Logs include inputs, resolution decisions, and outputs.
- Deterministic ordering and no network usage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:39.658643959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T09:18:40.431951432Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:18:40.431888685Z","close_reason":"Added ResourceLoader discovery/diagnostics tests; made resource dedupe deterministic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gn","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gn","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hr","title":"Spec: Unified JS+WASM capability model","description":"# Goal
Define a **single, coherent capability model** that applies equally to JS (PiJS) and WASM extensions, so security policy, logs, and tooling are identical across runtimes.

# Why
- Avoid divergent permission semantics between JS and WASM.
- Enable shared tooling (manifests, scanners, audit logs) and a consistent user mental model.
- JS-tier may execute wasm via PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry); capability semantics must remain identical.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Capability taxonomy: common names, scopes, and semantics across JS/WASM.
- Hostcall mapping: how JS `pi.*` calls and WASM hostcalls map to the same capabilities.
- Manifest alignment: how a single manifest expresses capabilities for both runtimes.
- Logging schema alignment: identical fields and redaction rules.

# Constraints
- Must compose with `bd-37z` ABI.
- Must be compatible with WASM host (`bd-nom`) and JS runtime (`bd-123`).

# Tests This Enables
- Cross-runtime capability conformance tests.
- E2E security suite verifying identical enforcement paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:44.048778878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300434438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300367473Z","close_reason":"Unified JS+WASM capability model spec added","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hz","title":"Extension UI protocol integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"Background:
- Legacy spec defines extension_ui_request/extension_ui_response (confirm/select/input/editor/notify/etc.) and blocking semantics.
- EXTENSIONS.md mandates Node/Bun-free runtime with capability-gated connectors and deterministic logs.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement runtime plumbing so extension UI requests surface in interactive TUI (prompt/confirm/select/editor) with timeout + cancellation.
2) Implement RPC mode passthrough: emit extension_ui_request on stdout; accept extension_ui_response on stdin.
3) Enforce request/response ID mapping, default values, and timeouts; normalize errors.
4) Add conformance fixtures for each UI method + timeout/cancel edge cases; wire into tests.
5) Emit structured logs (pi.ext.log.v1) for UI requests/responses with correlation IDs.

Acceptance:
- A sample extension can invoke confirm/select/input/editor and receive responses in both interactive + RPC modes.
- Timeouts and cancellation propagate correctly without deadlocks.
- Fixtures pass and logs are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.808698612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834579243Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2i2u","title":"Unit tests: provider factory + OpenAI base URL normalization","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for src/providers/mod.rs factory selection and OpenAI base URL normalization.

# Scope / Deliverables
- create_provider returns Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini providers with expected name/api/model_id.
- Azure OpenAI path returns the explicit error (resource+deployment).
- Unknown provider returns Error::provider with clear message.
- normalize_openai_base handles:
  - base URL ending with /v1
  - already /chat/completions or /responses
  - trailing slashes and missing path

# No-Mock Rule
- Use real ModelEntry structs; no fake providers or network calls.

# Logging
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log input base URLs and normalized outputs.

# Acceptance
- 10+ tests covering factory selection and base URL normalization edge cases.

 {"id":"bd-2ypy","title":"Release: GitHub Releases binaries + checksums (linux/macOS/windows)","description":"# Goal
Publish versioned `pi` binaries as GitHub Release artifacts for major OS/arch targets, with checksums and reproducible metadata.

# Background
Crates.io is great for Rust users, but many users want a single download. The original pi-mono shipped via npm/Bun; for Rust parity we should offer GitHub Releases.

# Target Artifacts (initial)
- Linux x86_64 (gnu)
- macOS arm64
- macOS x86_64 (optional if CI cost is acceptable)
- Windows x86_64 (msvc)

# Requirements
- Artifacts include:
  - `pi` (or `pi.exe`)
  - `SHA256SUMS` (or per-file `.sha256`)
  - optional: `SBOM` or `build manifest` (nice-to-have)
- Release notes reference the same changelog content used in `CHANGELOG.md`.
- No secrets embedded; ensure redaction policies are respected.

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a single workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) triggered on tags (`vX.Y.Z`).
- Use `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly` and cache build artifacts.
- Consider `cargo-dist` only if it simplifies rather than adds complexity; otherwise keep a minimal bespoke workflow.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tagging `vX.Y.Z` produces a GitHub Release with downloadable binaries.
- [ ] Checksums are published and verified in CI.
- [ ] Release notes are consistent with `CHANGELOG.md`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:03.490502891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T00:48:53.910167038Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:48:53.910101907Z","close_reason":"Add .github/workflows/release.yml for GitHub Release binaries + SHA256SUMS (commit bc9b34f)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ypy","depends_on_id":"bd-1ve4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ypy","depends_on_id":"bd-22k8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ypy","depends_on_id":"bd-dj27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ypy","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yw7","title":"Interactive: double-escape action (tree/fork) per settings","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy “double escape” shortcut:
- Press Escape twice to open `/tree` (default) or `/fork` depending on setting.

# Legacy Spec
`legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md`:
- `doubleEscapeAction`: `"tree"` or `"fork"`

# Current Gap
`src/config.rs` has `double_escape_action` but interactive doesn’t implement double-escape behavior.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Escape twice (within a small time window) triggers the configured action.
- [ ] Single Escape retains its normal meaning (cancel/abort).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:42:10.778142310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:39:20.910436385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:39:20.910372416Z","close_reason":"Implemented double-escape action + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yw7","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yw7","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yw7","depends_on_id":"bd-lqec","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yw7","depends_on_id":"bd-x85o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-30hr","title":"Proptest: truncation invariants (lines/bytes/head-tail)","description":"## Goal
Add property-based tests for truncation logic to ensure invariants hold across random inputs.

## Targets
- Shared truncation utilities used by tools (`read`, `bash`, `grep`, `find`, `ls`).

## Invariants to encode
- Output never exceeds configured byte/line budgets.
- Head/tail truncation markers appear when (and only when) truncation occurs.
- Truncation is stable: applying truncation twice yields the same output.
- UTF-8 validity is preserved (no broken codepoints).
- Line numbering (where applicable) remains consistent and monotonic.

## Acceptance
- proptest suite runs quickly in CI (< ~1s per test module).
- Any failing minimal cases are promoted into a deterministic unit test.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:04:57.719092795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:31:55.758142598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30hr","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30u","title":"Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios","description":"# Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios

## Goal
Create recorded cassettes for all Anthropic provider test scenarios.

## Scenarios to Record

### Happy Path Scenarios
1. **Simple text response** - Single text block, stop reason "stop"
2. **Multi-paragraph response** - Multiple text deltas
3. **Extended thinking** - thinking_start/delta/end + text response
4. **Tool call single** - Single tool_call in response
5. **Tool call multiple** - Multiple tool_calls in single response
6. **Tool result processing** - tool_result message handling

### Error Scenarios
7. **Rate limit (429)** - With retry-after header
8. **Auth failure (401)** - Invalid API key
9. **Forbidden (403)** - Resource access denied
10. **Bad request (400)** - Malformed request
11. **Server error (500)** - Internal server error
12. **Overloaded (529)** - Anthropic overloaded

### Edge Cases
13. **Empty response** - No content blocks
14. **Very long response** - Hits max_tokens
15. **Unicode content** - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
16. **Large tool call args** - Big JSON in arguments
17. **Stream interruption** - Partial response simulation

### Extended Thinking Variants
18. **Thinking only** - No final text, just thinking
19. **Thinking with tool calls** - Thinking then tool use
20. **Thinking budget exceeded** - Hits thinking token limit

## Recording Process
```bash
# Set API key (not committed)
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Record mode
VCR_MODE=record cargo test provider_streaming::anthropic

# Verify cassettes created
ls tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_*.json
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) during recording runs.
- Log cassette name/path, model, prompt summary hash, and redaction summary.
- Capture command line + env (redacted) used to record each cassette.

## Determinism + Redaction
- Keep prompts/tool schemas identical across recordings; store prompt hashes in a manifest.
- Ensure API keys and any sensitive fields are redacted.
- Record with fixed model versions + params (temperature, max_tokens, thinking level).

## Files Created
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_simple_text.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_extended_thinking.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_tool_call.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_rate_limit.json
- ... (20 cassettes total)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_manifest.json (scenario → prompt hash + params)

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure must exist)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20 cassettes recorded
- [ ] All scenarios covered
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Cassettes valid JSON
- [ ] Manifest includes prompt hashes + params
- [ ] Playback matches recording
- [ ] Recording logs captured with redacted env","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:42.752221104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:29.773478724Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30u","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30u","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"bd-30u","author":"codex","text":"Starting bd-30u. Plan: build Anthropic VCR scenario runner + manifest writer; record cassettes when API key available. Note: MCP Agent Mail unavailable in this environment, will log progress here.","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:09:46Z"},{"id":129,"issue_id":"bd-30u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed provider streaming test compilation issues: added Provider trait imports to all provider test files (anthropic, azure, gemini, openai) and replaced expect_err with idiomatic let-else pattern for error handling. Tests now compile and skip gracefully in VCR playback mode without cassettes.","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:14Z"},{"id":130,"issue_id":"bd-30u","author":"codex","text":"Implemented VCR-backed provider_streaming test harness + 20 Anthropic scenarios (record/playback). Added manifest writer (tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_manifest.json) in record mode, logging + skip-on-missing-cassette with VCR_STRICT gate. Recording is blocked here because ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (and other provider keys) are unset. Need real keys to run VCR_MODE=record and generate cassettes.","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30zd","title":"Docs: README - correct asupersync vs tokio migration status","description":"## Goal
Update `README.md` so it accurately reflects the current implementation state.

## Background
`README.md` currently states that tokio “handles the main runtime” and that the asupersync migration is ongoing. In the current codebase, `Cargo.toml` already uses `asupersync` as the async runtime and there are no tokio/reqwest references in `src/`.

## Scope
- Remove/replace statements implying tokio is still in use.
- Ensure the “Foundation Libraries / asupersync” section describes the *current* architecture (runtime + HTTP/TLS + cancellation model).
- Update any examples or wording that imply reqwest/tokio.
- Keep extension-related sections intact (we are not changing extension plans here).

## Acceptance
- README contains no claims that contradict `Cargo.toml` + `src/main.rs`.
- README’s architecture description matches how the binary actually runs today.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:03:14.131250661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T23:13:02.913549237Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T23:13:02.913482833Z","close_reason":"Completed: README now reflects asupersync runtime/HTTP + SSE parser; removed tokio/reqwest migration claims","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30zd","depends_on_id":"bd-gor5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Acceptance:
